channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated by AI

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel sent Iron Dome and troops to UAE during Iran war
item.description: Axios reports Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery and personnel to the UAE amid the Iran conflict, deepening defense ties between the countries.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Tuareg rebels announce Russian Africa Corps withdrawal from Kidal
item.description: Tuareg rebels in northern Mali reached a deal for the Russian Africa Corps to withdraw from Kidal as fighting continues between the army and separatist forces.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Israeli PM says Hezbollah actions are threatening the ceasefire with Lebanon as sirens warn of suspected drone attacks in northern Israel.

item.title: 🛢️ Russia warns months needed to rebalance oil market after Hormuz blockade
item.description: Russian Deputy PM Alexander Novak warned of deep energy crisis as the Hormuz blockade prevents large volumes of oil from reaching global markets.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China positioned to benefit from energy-driven renewables race
item.description: The US-Iran war may have inadvertently triggered a global energy transition favoring Beijing's green technology leadership, analysts say.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel President Herzog seeks plea deal over pardon for Netanyahu
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's pardon request, instead pushing for mediation that could lead to a plea deal in his corruption trial. Netanyahu faces charges in three cases.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Car bomb explodes outside Belfast police station in suspected dissident attack
item.description: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry, south Belfast, after a delivery vehicle was hijacked. Northern Ireland police called it a "miracle" no one was injured and suspect dissident republicans, possibly the New IRA.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia announces M armored vehicle deal amid weapons buildup
item.description: Australia will spend more than $800 million on armored vehicles, adding to its recent weapons purchases as the Middle East conflict drives concerns about regional security and supply chain vulnerabilities.

item.title: 🇴🇲 Iran FM Araqchi discusses Hormuz security and war end efforts with Oman
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi held talks in Oman on Sunday to discuss security in the Strait of Hormuz and broader Gulf waters, as well as diplomatic efforts to end the Iran-U.S. conflict. The meeting comes as US-Iran peace talks remain stalled.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles state visit under security review after Washington shooting
item.description: Buckingham Palace is holding discussions with US authorities about whether the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner will affect security planning for King Charles's state visit to the US this week. The King expressed relief that Trump was unharmed.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner after gunman breaches security
item.description: A 31-year-old California man armed with a shotgun, handgun and knives charged a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton, exchanging gunfire with officers before being tackled. One Secret Service agent was injured but survived thanks to a bulletproof vest. Trump was safely evacuated and later addressed the nation from the White House.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali separatists join al-Qaida-linked militants in coordinated attacks on army
item.description: Tuareg-led Azawad Liberation Front said it partnered with JNIM to launch one of the biggest coordinated attacks on Malian forces in Bamako and four other cities. At least 16 wounded. First time separatists explicitly confirmed alliance with jihadists.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan races to revive stalled US-Iran talks after Trump scraps envoys visit
item.description: Pakistani officials scrambling to restart negotiations after Trump canceled his envoys trip to Islamabad. Iranian FM Araghchi met Oman sultan to discuss Strait of Hormuz mechanism. Ceasefire holding but economic fallout growing as Brent crude up 50% since war began.

item.title: 🇺🇦☢️ Sixteen killed in Russia-Ukraine strikes on Chernobyl anniversary
item.description: At least 16 people killed in strikes across Ukraine and Russia as the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster highlighted nuclear risks. Ukraine struck a major oil refinery in Yaroslavl deep inside Russia. Zelenskyy warned Russia is bringing world to brink of man-made disaster.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in coordinated attacks
item.description: Mali's Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a coordinated attack on the garrison town of Kati, amid simultaneous attacks across the West African country, marking a major escalation in regional instability.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇲🇲 China backs Myanmar's push to ease diplomatic isolation, fight telecoms scams
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded a five-day Southeast Asia tour by backing Myanmar's efforts to reduce its diplomatic isolation and crack down on telecoms fraud operations.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇺🇸 Two US citizens among 19 killed in Philippines clashes with communist rebels
item.description: Two American citizens were among 19 suspected New People's Army guerrillas killed during clashes with Philippine military forces on April 19 in Negros Occidental province.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU commissioners arrive in Kyiv to secure Chornobyl site
item.description: EU Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis and Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen visited Kyiv on April 26 to discuss making the Chornobyl nuclear site safe again, marking continued European support for Ukraine's nuclear safety.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Syria holds first public trial of Assad-era officials in Damascus
item.description: Syria opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad's rule in Damascus on Sunday, marking a significant step in post-civil war accountability efforts.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Mexico: US agents killed in crash not authorized for local operations
item.description: Two US federal agents, reportedly CIA, died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Mexican authorities say they were not permitted to operate in the country.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇺🇸 Trump cancels US envoys trip to Pakistan for Iran war talks
item.description: US President cancelled the delegation trip led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner after Iran said there were no plans for direct meeting. Peace prospects dim further.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Palestinians hold local elections in West Bank and Gaza
item.description: Local elections conducted in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups did not participate.

item.title: 🇦🇫 Afghanistan urges Afghans stuck in Qatar to return home
item.description: Taliban government calls on Afghans who helped US forces and are now stranded in Qatar to return to Afghanistan.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuela prisoner release scheme nearing end, rights groups warn
item.description: More than 500 political prisoners remain jailed as amnesty program winds down amid criticism from human rights organizations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Negotiator Trip
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned trip by envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks, citing inadequate Iranian proposals. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters a stalemate phase.
